_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of the POC’s, databases, tools and applications necessary to establish environment preparation and surveillance products.
reference_document_element_description: Manage development, staging, and production environments for data applications, including environment configuration, dependency specification, secrets management, and environment parity maintenance.
comments: DCWF ksa intersects with WIDAI TF domain. TF-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-K-010 (DQ, cosine: 0.399), DQ-T-005 (DQ, cosine: 0.381).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.0/10 (raw 0.500). Shared vocabulary: [applications, environment]. DCWF-only terms [databases, establish, knowledge, necessary, poc, preparation] vs WIDAI-only terms [configuration, data, dependency, development, environments, maintenance] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.858 (P=0.857, R=0.859). Balanced P/R — the 16-word DCWF element and 20-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Knowledge of the POC’s, databases, tools and applications necessary to establish" and "Manage development, staging, and production environments for data applications," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.433 < STS 0.500 (gap -0.067). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [databases, establish, knowledge] and [configuration, data, dependency] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 9.7%. Shared tokens: [applications, environment]. DCWF-only: [databases, establish, knowledge, necessary, poc, preparation]. WIDAI-only: [configuration, data, dependency, development, environments, maintenance].
